rulings.Case Title: UNIFUND CCR PARTNERS, A PARTNERSHIP DOING BUSINESS VS. ANNETTE D BONIFACIO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order For Sale Of Dwelling
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 3, 2023, line 1. ASSIGNEE NDS, LLC Motion For Order For Sale Of Dwelling is GRANTED. The property may be sold for not less than 90% of 1,900,000, subject to senior liens and immediate payment of tax liens. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JAMIE LEE VS. PATRICIA TELLERIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Discovery, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Jamie Lee And Request For Trial Continuance
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 3, 2023, line 7. DEFENDANT PATRICIA TELLERIA Defendant'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Discovery, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Jamie Lee And Request For Trial Continuance is GRANTED. Plaintiff shall provide complete verified responses without objections to Special Interrogatories (Set One); Form Interrogatories - Unlawful Detainer (Set One); and Requests for Production of Documents (Set One) within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Trial is ordered off calendar. Any party may file a request to set after the above responses are provided. Sanctions granted in the amount of $700 to be paid within 5 days of notice of entry of order. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DOUGLAS CAMERON ET AL VS. BJORN BAKSTAND ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 3, 2023, line 9. DEFENDANT BJORN BAKSTAND, CHEYENNE BAKSTAD DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that required information was omitted from the Notice. Whether Plaintiff failed to comply with their continuous obligation under SFRO is an issue not properly addressed on a demurrer. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC VS. RICHARD PEREZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 3, 2023, line 10. DEFENDANT RICHARD PEREZ MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is DENIED. Motion is set for hearing in violation of CCP 1167.4. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that an employee of the Plaintiff is a party to the action within the meaning of CCP 414.10. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
